preamble: The shift from the voiced postalveolar fricative [ʒ] (zh) to the palatal approximant [j] is a process of gliding or lenition. This transformation involves the loss of fricative turbulence while maintaining voicing and the palatal place of articulation, a hallmark of yeísmo variants in Spanish and other Romance developments.

examples.note: In many dialects, the 'strong' palatals [ʒ] or [ʝ] reduce to a pure palatal glide [j].
Stages in the lenition of historical palatalized sounds where fricatives weakened into glides before elision.

sources: Penny, R. (2002). A History of the Spanish Language.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
